title_en: Does Anyone Know What ‘Wellness’ Means Anymore?
title_zh: 如今还有人知道“健康养生”到底是什么意思吗？

paragraphs.en: Yes or no: Do you have any idea what “wellness” is? Depending on where you live and which online rabbit holes you’ve tripped into, your answer to that question—and your actual definition of wellness—may vary widely.
paragraphs.zh: 是非题：你知道“健康养生”是什么吗？这取决于你住在哪里、以及你在网上掉进了哪些“兔子洞”，你对这个问题的答案，以及你对“wellness/健康养生”的真实理解，可能会大不相同。

paragraphs.en: The line between science and wellness has been blurred beyond recognition. WIRED is here to help.
paragraphs.zh: 科学与健康养生之间的界线已经模糊到无法辨认。WIRED 来帮你理清。

paragraphs.en: And yet, we’re in a moment where wellness is the holy grail du jour, sometimes at the expense of our actual health. There’s the softer version of wellness, one characterized by some combination of smoothie consumption and aspirational TikTok videos. Then there are the more hard-line (and health hazardous) variations involving everything from (basically) bleach drinking to parasite cleanses to “wellness farms” designed to wean you off antidepressants. Regardless of which wellness doctrine you ascribe to, one thing is clear: The business of wellness, now worth, by one estimate, more than $6.3 trillion worldwide, is booming.
paragraphs.zh: 然而，我们正处在这样一个阶段：wellness 成了当下的圣杯，但有时是以牺牲真正健康为代价。它有更“温和”的版本，通常包含果昔消费和充满理想化滤镜的 TikTok 视频；也有更激进、甚至有害健康的版本，内容从几乎是在鼓励喝漂白剂，到“排除寄生虫排毒”，再到“健康农场”——这些地方旨在让你停用抗抑郁药。无论你信奉哪种 wellness 学说，有一点很清楚：据某项估计，价值超过 6.3 万亿美元的 wellness 生意如今正席卷全球并持续扩张。

paragraphs.en: So too is the politicization. Some of the most prominent figures in the nebulous world of wellness are now, of course, firmly ensconced in key US government roles—and are using their authority to wreak havoc on the lives of Americans. In his first few months leading US Health and Human Services, anti-vax poster boy Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has fired every member of the CDC’s advisory committee on immunization practices, replacing them with a mixed bag of credible experts and “vaccine skeptics”; laid off thousands of civil servants and cut billions in research funding to colleges and public health departments; and used his White House perch to take shots of raw milk on camera. All in the name of Kennedy’s anti-science bid to Make America Healthy Again.
paragraphs.zh: 政治化也是同样一股潮流。如今，wellness 这片模糊领域中的一些最知名人物，当然已经牢牢坐进了美国政府关键岗位，并在利用他们的权力对美国人的生活造成严重破坏。在刚刚上任担任美国卫生与公众服务部领导的反疫苗代言人罗伯特·F·肯尼迪三世（Robert F. Kennedy Jr.）的头几个月里，他解雇了 CDC 疫苗接种实践咨询委员会的全部成员，改由一批“可信专家”和“疫苗怀疑者”混合组成的新阵容接替；他裁减了数千名公务员，并削减了大学与公共卫生部门数十亿美元的科研经费；他还在白宫“坐擁”地位下当着镜头喝下生牛奶。一切都冠冕堂皇地打着肯尼迪反科学、让美国再次健康（Make America Healthy Again）的旗号。

paragraphs.en: But don’t despair just yet. As you’ll read in this issue, robust, invigorating, truly innovative research is still uncovering new tools and therapies, and solving once intractable medical mysteries, in the US and around the world. To that end, we’ll take you inside the forefront of AI-fueled drug discovery and the weakened state of cancer research. There’s lighter fare, too, like a look at animal wellness hobbies, including iceberg exfoliation. And our trusted Gear team is here to tell you which out-there health products are really worth your hard-earned money. As a recent trip to Paris reminded me, wellness doesn’t need to be that complicated. If only we could all eat fresh food, walk a lot, and enjoy a vice or two at an outdoor table. Pair that with a heartfelt vow not to drink anything resembling bleach, and I promise, you’re halfway to peak performance already.
paragraphs.zh: 但别就此失望。正如你在本期中将看到的，仍有扎实、有活力且真正创新的研究在美国及全球持续发现新的工具与疗法，并破解过去被视为难以攻克的医学谜团。为此，我们将带你走进以人工智能驱动的新药研发前沿，以及癌症研究陷入停滞的现实。也有更轻松的内容，比如一则关于动物健康爱好的报道，其中包括冰山磨砂疗法。我们的资深 Gear 团队也会告诉你，哪些看起来“很离谱”的健康产品确实值得你辛苦赚来的钱。最近一次去巴黎的经历提醒我，健康并不必弄得那么复杂。只要我们都能多吃点新鲜食物、多走路、在户外餐桌上偶尔放松一下，情况就已经大不相同了。再加上真心发誓不要喝任何像漂白剂味道的东西，我向你保证，你离最佳状态其实已经走过一半路了。
